---
title: "Project Memory"
format: gfm
---

## Product Direction

- Build a native macOS app with a straightforward desktop UI.
- Focus on reviewing temporary files and large files, then moving them to Trash rather than deleting them permanently.
- Keep the app practical and low-friction for everyday cleanup.

## Current Product Behavior

- The app is a native AppKit macOS application, not a web wrapper.
- The primary deliverable is a signed `.app` bundle at `dist/Mac Drive Cleaner.app`.
- The packaged archive is `dist/Mac Drive Cleaner.zip`.
- The app should surface temporary files and large files in one easy-to-scan table with simple bulk actions.
- File removal must go through macOS Trash APIs so cleanup stays reversible.
- The app menu should support standard macOS behavior including `Cmd+H` to hide and `Cmd+Q` to quit.
- Clicking the app icon or Dock icon should restore the main window if the app is already running but hidden.

## UI Notes

- Keep the interface simple and desktop-native.
- Favor direct labels and obvious actions over clever wording.
- Avoid clutter, permanent-delete language, or anything that makes the app feel risky.
- Preserve the bundled custom app icon in `App/Resources/AppIcon.icns`.

## Repo Rules

- Do not add any "made by codex", "built with codex", or similar AI attribution text anywhere in the product, repo, issues, pull requests, or release artifacts.
- Use Dave F. Murray ownership and identity only.
- When tagging or mentioning on GitHub, use `@davefmurray`.
- Keep issue, PR, and bug language direct and product-focused.

## Build And Packaging

- Build the app with `./scripts/build_app.sh`.
- Build the distributable archive with `./scripts/package_app.sh`.
- Keep the build pipeline compatible with the local macOS Objective-C/AppKit toolchain used by this repo.
- Preserve bundle signing and extended-attribute cleanup in the build flow so the `.app` launches cleanly from Finder.

## GitHub Workflow

- Track meaningful product and packaging work in GitHub issues and PRs.
- When making fixes, keep the local repo, merged `main`, and `dist/` artifacts in sync.
- Comment on issues and PRs with direct validation notes when behavior is verified locally.
- Keep all issue and PR language under the `@davefmurray` identity only.

## Delivery Notes

- Prefer shipping a usable `.app` bundle in `dist/`.
- Prefer build scripts that work with the local macOS toolchain available in this repo.
- Keep deletion reversible by sending files to Trash.
